title: External Integrations, Distributed Effects, and Resilience · Product Evidence Studio
summary: A guided product team extends controlled integration and resilience evidence while preserving every earlier problem, accessibility, security, testing, operations, and recovery requirement. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of controlled integration and resilience evidence while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Document provider purpose, authority, data, credentials, terms, version, quota, availability, cost, accessibility, replacement, and exit boundaries.
Wrap external behavior behind owned contracts and test requests, responses, errors, limits, version drift, and semantic mismatch with controlled fixtures.
Assign effect identity, idempotency, ordering, concurrency, retry, timeout, unknown-outcome, compensation, and reconciliation rules.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# capstone-project cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: capstone-project
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Integration Authority
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A critical provider changes schema during an outage while retries duplicate effects, cached fallback misleads users, and no vendor-exit rehearsal exists. The workshop variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Document provider purpose, authority, data, credentials, terms, version, quota, availability, cost, accessibility, replacement, and exit boundaries.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Integration Authority.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for document provider purpose, authority, data, credentials, terms, version, quota, availability, cost, accessibility, replacement, and exit boundaries. For Commit to the mission model · Workshop Integration Authority, keep Integration Authority connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: A critical provider changes schema during an outage while retries duplicate effects, cached fallback misleads users, and no vendor-exit rehearsal exists. The workshop variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Document provider purpose, authority, data, credentials, terms, version, quota, availability, cost, accessibility, replacement, and exit boundaries.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Integration Authority condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: GOV.UK Service Manual discovery and alpha guidance — Controls problem-first discovery, affected-user and constraint research, measures of success, riskiest-assumption prototypes, and evidence-based stop decisions before production building.

hints: For Commit to the mission model · Workshop Integration Authority, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Integration Authority precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Commit to the mission model · Workshop Integration Authority, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A critical provider changes schema during an outage while retries duplicate effects, cached fallback misleads users, and no vendor-exit rehearsal exists. The workshop variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Document provider purpose, authority, data, credentials, terms, version, quota, availability, cost, accessibility, replacement, and exit boundaries.

For Commit to the mission model · Workshop Integration Authority, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: An API key and SDK establish permission to use a service.

options.text: State the Integration Authority preconditions, preserve earlier contracts, and name the trace or changed-input result that will decide success.
An API key and SDK establish permission to use a service. That claim does not survive the commit to the mission model evidence boundary.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

stimulus.title: Vendor Exit case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A critical provider changes schema during an outage while retries duplicate effects, cached fallback misleads users, and no vendor-exit rehearsal exists.
